title: 10 best herbal teas for itchy skin
intro_desc: Herbal teas for itchy skin are infusions made from various plants and herbs that have anti-inflammatory, soothing, and healing properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort of itchy skin. Chamomile tea, for instance, contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce skin irritation and inflammation. Its antioxidant properties can also help speed up the skin's healing process. Another example is nettle tea, which is rich in minerals, vitamins, and antioxidants that can help soothe itchy skin and reduce redness and inflammation. By drinking these herbal teas or using them as a compress, individuals can find relief from itchy skin, improving their overall quality of life by reducing discomfort, promoting better sleep, and enhancing their confidence and well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The flavonoids present in yarrow tea can reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin, providing immediate relief from itching and discomfort. Additionally, yarrow's antiseptic qualities help to prevent infections that can exacerbate itchiness, promoting a healthy balance of bacteria on the skin. Drinking this tea regularly can also help to strengthen the skin's natural barrier function, reducing its susceptibility to irritation and itchiness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation and itching associated with skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of yarrow tea, including its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, can help combat infections that cause itchy skin and promote wound healing.

Soothing and calming effects: Yarrow tea's soothing and calming effects on the skin, partly due to its flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, can provide relief from itching and irritation, making it an effective natural remedy for skin conditions like atopic dermatitis and psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties that help soothe itchy skin.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, these flowers have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce itching and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves, flowers, and stems from sustainable sources.
Combine one teaspoon of dried yarrow or two teaspoons of fresh yarrow with one cup boiling water.
Steep the mixture for five to ten minutes in a large mug or teapot to release medicinal properties.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding solids and excess herbs.
Enjoy hot or cold within 24 hours of preparation, with honey or lemon if desired, for optimal benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with itchy skin because its active ingredients, such as mucilage, soothe and calm irritated skin. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and swelling, while its emollient qualities moisturize and hydrate the skin, providing instant relief from itching and discomfort. Additionally, the antioxidants present in marshmallow tea help to protect the skin from environmental stressors and damage, promoting overall skin health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow tea contains mucilages, which are thick, gel-like substances that soothe and protect irritated skin, reducing itchiness by creating a barrier between the skin and irritants.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and itching caused by skin conditions like eczema, acne, or sunburn.
Antioxidants: Marshmallow tea's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to itchiness, promoting overall skin health and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which soothes and calms itchy skin by forming a protective barrier.
Leaves: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and itching caused by skin irritation.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried marshmallow roots, measuring 2-3 tablespoons per 8 oz serving.
Boil water and combine with marshmallow root in a teapot or infuser for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Add honey to taste, adjusting sweetness levels as desired, and serve hot or chilled.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock teas helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The tea's flavonoids and saponins work to reduce inflammation, while its antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate itchiness. Additionally, burdock's antiseptic properties help to prevent infection, reducing the risk of further irritation and itching. As a result, drinking burdock teas can provide relief from itchy skin conditions like eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Burdock root contains flavonoids and polyphenols that reduce inflammation and soothe itchy skin by reducing redness and swelling.
Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants present in burdock tea help combat oxidative stress and free radicals, which can contribute to itchy skin conditions like eczema and psoriasis.

Antimicrobial and antibacterial properties: Burdock's antimicrobial and antibacterial compounds can help fight off infections that may cause or exacerbate itchy skin conditions, such as acne, folliculitis, and cellulitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots are rich in mucilage, a gel-like substance that soothes and calms irritated skin, providing relief from itchiness.
Seeds: The seeds contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy skin conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried burdock roots, choosing those that are free of damage or signs of rot.
Rinse the burdock roots with clean water to remove any dirt or debris, then chop them into small pieces.
Measure out one teaspoon of chopped burdock root for every eight ounces of boiling water, ensuring an optimal dosage.
Steep the chopped burdock root in boiling water for 10-15 minutes, allowing the medicinal properties to infuse and release.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and serving the herbal tea hot or chilled as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The tea's antioxidants also help to reduce redness and swelling, providing relief from itching caused by conditions such as eczema and psoriasis. Additionally, the tea's antiseptic properties help to prevent infection, promoting healthy skin and reducing the urge to scratch and further irritate the skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation in the skin and alleviate itching.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tea, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, neutralize free radicals that can contribute to skin irritation and itching.
Calming effects: Pot marigold tea has a soothing and calming effect on the skin, which helps to reduce itching, redness, and inflammation by relaxing the skin tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help to reduce itching and inflammation caused by skin irritations.
Flowers: The bright yellow petals contain flavonoids, which have antihistaminic properties that alleviate itchiness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers from your garden or store-bought supply.
Rinse the flowers with filtered water to remove any impurities or debris.
Steep one teaspoon of the flowers in 250ml of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the mixture using a tea infuser or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Serve the tea hot, sweetened with honey if desired, and enjoy within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile teas helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The apigenin present in chamomile tea binds to GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system. This calming effect helps to reduce itching and inflammation caused by conditions such as eczema, psoriasis, and contact dermatitis. Additionally, chamomile's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial and fungal infections that can contribute to itchy skin, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a healthy complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: German Chamomile contains apigenin, luteolin, and other flavonoids that reduce inflammation and itching by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Antihistamine effects: The herb's active compounds, including apigenin and isohamnetol, have antihistamine properties, which help to relieve itchy skin caused by allergic reactions or histamine release.

Soothing and calming effect: German Chamomile's essential oils, such as bisabolol and chamazulene, possess a sedative and anti-irritant action that helps to calm the skin, reducing itching and redness associated with itchy skin conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of German chamomile are used to create a soothing and calming effect on itchy skin, reducing inflammation and itching.
Flowers: The flowers of German chamomile contain apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce redness and itching associated with itchy skin.
remedy_recipe: Measure one tablespoon of dried chamomile flowers for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Pour the measured water over the dried chamomile flowers in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes or until the tea reaches desired strength and flavor.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Enjoy the soothing and calming effects of the herbal german chamomile tea immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail teas helps with itchy skin because of its high silica content, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The antioxidants present in horsetail tea also help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin cell growth, reducing the occurrence of itchy rashes and dryness. Additionally, horsetail's antimicrobial properties can help to eliminate bacterial infections that may be contributing to itchiness, providing long-lasting relief from itchy skin conditions.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Horsetail tea contains saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, helping to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing itchiness.
Silica: Horsetail is rich in silica, a mineral that helps to strengthen skin tissues and improve skin elasticity, reducing the appearance of dry, itchy skin and promoting overall skin health.

Antioxidants: Horsetail tea contains antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, reducing itchiness and promoting a healthy skin barrier.
remedy_parts: Roots: Horsetail roots are rich in silica, which helps to soothe and calm itchy skin by reducing inflammation and improving skin elasticity.
Bark: The bark of the horsetail plant contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to relieve itching and reduce redness caused by skin irritations.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried equisetum arvense plant material from trusted sources weighing approximately one ounce per quart of water.
Combine the plant material with boiling water in a large pot and reduce heat to simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before transferring it to an airtight container refrigerate at 40°f or below for up to 72 hours.
Serve the herbal horsetail tea hot or chilled sweeten with honey if desired and drink 1-2 cups per day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with itching. The tea also contains antioxidants that soothe and calm irritated skin, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, fennel's natural antibacterial properties help to prevent infections that can exacerbate itchy skin conditions. Drinking fennel tea regularly may provide long-term relief for individuals experiencing chronic itchiness due to various skin conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and polyphenols, which can help reduce inflammation and itching associated with skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis.
Antioxidant activity: Fennel's antioxidant properties can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itchy skin and other skin issues.
Soothing and calming effects: Fennel tea has a soothing and calming effect on the skin, which can help reduce itching and discomfort associated with conditions like hives and bug bites.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated skin.
Seeds: The seeds of the fennel plant contain a number of compounds with known antipruritic effects, helping to relieve itching and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Measure one tablespoon of dried fennel fruit foeniculum vulgare for every eight ounces.
Bring filtered water to boil in a medium-sized pot and remove from heat immediately.
Add the measured fennel fruit to the boiling water and let steep for seven to nine minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or mug.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving or store in an airtight container refrigerated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling that often accompany itchy skin conditions. The tea's flavonoids also have a soothing effect on the skin, calming irritations and discomfort caused by dryness or rashes. Additionally, licorice root contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to possess antipruritic properties, actively combating itching sensations and providing relief from skin irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and itching in the skin.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in licorice tea, such as polyphenols, can help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itchy skin.
Soothing and moisturizing properties: Licorice root contains soothing compounds like glycyrrhizin, which can help calm irritated skin, reduce itching, and lock in moisture to soothe dry, itchy skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties, making them effective in soothing itchy skin.
Bark: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce itching and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the morning after sunrise.
Cleanse the roots with filtered water to remove impurities and debris thoroughly.
Cut the roots into small pieces and chop them into fine particles using a mortar and pestle slowly.
Steep 5-7 grams of chopped licorice root in 1 quart of boiling filtered water for 10-15 minutes quietly.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and serve hot or at room temperature promptly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English ivy teas helps with itchy skin because its antipruritic properties soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and relieving itching. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds also help to reduce redness and swelling, providing long-lasting relief from itchy skin conditions such as eczema, dermatitis, and psoriasis. Additionally, English ivy teas' antimicrobial properties may help to combat bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate itchiness, promoting healthy skin and a sense of calm.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine compounds: English Ivy tea contains antihistamine compounds like sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce itching and inflammation by blocking the release of histamines, a chemical that causes allergic reactions.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in English Ivy tea have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe itchy skin and reduce redness and swelling associated with conditions like eczema or dermatitis.

Soothing saponins: The saponins found in English Ivy tea have a gentle, non-irritating effect on the skin, making them effective at calming itches and reducing dryness and irritation without stripping the skin of its natural oils.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm itchiness on the skin.
Bark: Contains flavonoids and saponins that have antipruritic properties, making it effective in reducing itching sensations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh english ivy leaves by pinching off a few stems from healthy plants daily.
Rinse the harvested leaves with cool water to remove dirt and debris gently.
Chop the cleaned english ivy leaves into small pieces weighing approximately one teaspoon per cup.
Steep the chopped leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes or until the desired strength is achieved.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding solids immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St john's wort teas helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tea contains flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and itching caused by skin conditions such as eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. Additionally, the antioxidants in St John's Wort help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort. By addressing underlying causes of itchiness, this tea can provide relief from chronic and uncomfortable skin symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antipruritic properties: St. John's Wort tea has anti-itch properties that can help alleviate itching sensations, making it an effective natural remedy for itchy skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds can reduce inflammation in the skin, which is often a contributing factor to itchy skin conditions, helping to soothe and calm irritated skin.

Antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort tea contains antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness, irritation, and itchiness of the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, the roots of St John's Wort have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm itchy skin.
Leaves: Containing a high concentration of essential oils, the leaves of St John's Wort can be used to make teas that provide quick relief from itching and scratching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers, choosing those that are bright yellow and have no signs of mold or mildew.
Rinse the harvested herbs with clean water to remove any dirt or debris, then gently pat dry with a paper towel.
Combine 2 tablespoons of dried st john's wort herbs with 1 quart of boiling water in a large glass pot, allowing it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids and reserve the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool before serving, then store it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze for later use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat itchy skin with herbal teas is by using them as a compress. Chamomile and calendula teas are particularly effective, as they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce irritation and itching. To prepare, steep 1-2 tea bags in boiling water for 10-15 minutes, then let it cool. Once cooled, soak a clean cloth in the tea and apply it to the affected area for 10-15 minutes, several times a day. This can help soothe and relieve itchiness, while also promoting skin healing.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy skin that are treated with herbal teas include eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. Chamomile tea is often used for its anti-inflammatory properties to soothe irritated skin, while nettle tea can help alleviate allergic reactions that cause itching. Green tea, rich in antioxidants, can reduce inflammation and improve skin health. Licorice root tea is known to have anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating effects, making it beneficial for various skin conditions. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any herbal remedy.
